---
title: Pages
---

The page template wraps the entire rendered body in a document shell: the `<html>` and `<head>` for HTML, the `\documentclass` and preamble for LaTeX, the equivalent for Typst.


# Customizing page templates

To customize the page layout for a format, create a template file in your project:

- `templates/html/page.html`: HTML page wrapper
- `templates/html/page.css`: default stylesheet (HTML only)
- `templates/latex/page.tex`: LaTeX document wrapper
- `templates/typst/page.typ`: Typst document wrapper
- `templates/common/page.jinja`: format-agnostic (with conditionals)

Resolution order:

1. Plugin `templates/` directory (in plugin order)
2. `templates/{target}/page.{ext}` (target-specific)
3. `templates/{base}/page.{ext}` (base-specific)
4. `templates/common/page.jinja` (format-agnostic)
5. Legacy: `_calepin/templates/calepin.{format}`
6. Built-in (compiled into the binary)


# Page template variables

All page templates receive these variables:

## Content

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{body}}`{% endraw %} | Rendered document content |
| {% raw %}`{{toc}}`{% endraw %} | Table of contents (format-specific) |
| {% raw %}`{{preamble}}`{% endraw %} | Extra preamble content (meta tags, etc.) |

## Metadata

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{plain_title}}`{% endraw %} | Title as plain text (markdown stripped) |
| {% raw %}`{{title}}`{% endraw %} | Title with inline markdown rendered |
| {% raw %}`{{author}}`{% endraw %} | Author(s), comma-joined |
| {% raw %}`{{date}}`{% endraw %} | Date string |
| {% raw %}`{{generator}}`{% endraw %} | `"calepin X.Y.Z"` |

## Pre-rendered blocks

These variables contain already-rendered markup, produced through overridable element templates (see [Elements](elements.html)):

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{title_block}}`{% endraw %} | Title markup |
| {% raw %}`{{subtitle_block}}`{% endraw %} | Subtitle markup |
| {% raw %}`{{author_block}}`{% endraw %} | Author metadata (names, affiliations, ORCID) |
| {% raw %}`{{date_block}}`{% endraw %} | Date markup |
| {% raw %}`{{abstract_block}}`{% endraw %} | Abstract text |
| {% raw %}`{{keywords_block}}`{% endraw %} | Keywords list |
| {% raw %}`{{appendix_block}}`{% endraw %} | License, citation, copyright, funding |
| {% raw %}`{{bibliography_block}}`{% endraw %} | Bibliography section |

## HTML-specific

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{css}}`{% endraw %} | `<style>` tag with default CSS |
| {% raw %}`{{js}}`{% endraw %} | JavaScript block |
| {% raw %}`{{math_block}}`{% endraw %} | KaTeX/MathJax script tags |

## LaTeX-specific

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{bib_preamble}}`{% endraw %} | Bibliography preamble commands |
| {% raw %}`{{bib_end}}`{% endraw %} | Bibliography end commands |

## Custom fields

Any non-standard front matter field is available as a page template variable (and in document bodies via {% raw %}`{{ var.key }}`{% endraw %}). For example:

```yaml
---
title: My Report
custom_footer: "Confidential"
---
```

Then in `templates/html/page.html`:

```html
<footer>{{custom_footer}}</footer>
```

## Literal braces

Use {% raw %}`{{_lb}}` and `{{_rb}}`{% endraw %} to insert literal `{` and `}` characters in templates where they would otherwise conflict with Jinja syntax. This is especially useful in LaTeX templates:

```latex
\label{{_lb}}{{id}}{{_rb}}
```

renders as `\label{my-id}`.


# Examples

## Minimal HTML page

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<title>{{plain_title}}</title>
{{css}}
{{header_includes}}
</head>
<body>
{{title_block}}
{{author_block}}
{{toc}}
{{body}}
{{appendix_block}}
</body>
</html>
```

Save as `templates/html/page.html` and all HTML output in your project will use it.

## Blog post layout

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<title>{{plain_title}}</title>
{{css}}
</head>
<body>
<article>
  <header>
    <h1>{{title}}</h1>
    <time>{{date}}</time>
    <p class="author">{{author}}</p>
  </header>
  {{body}}
  {{appendix_block}}
</article>
</body>
</html>
```

## Custom CSS

To change the stylesheet without replacing the entire HTML template, create `templates/html/page.css`. This replaces the default styles but keeps the HTML structure unchanged.

```css
body {
  max-width: 40em;
  margin: 2em auto;
  font-family: Georgia, serif;
  line-height: 1.6;
}
```
